name: Google Digital Skills for Africa
bestFor: Beginners, jobseekers and small business owners
freeDetails: Free digital marketing and online business learning from Google.
requirements: Google account may be needed for progress tracking and certificates.
watchOut: Check the course page before promising a certificate because course availability can change.

name: Microsoft Learn South Africa
bestFor: Students, IT beginners and people building technical skills
freeDetails: Free learning paths, product training and selected virtual training events from Microsoft.
requirements: Microsoft account is useful for saving progress; some certifications or exams may cost money.
watchOut: Free learning content is not always the same as a free certification exam.

name: Vodacom Digital Skills Hub
bestFor: Young people and jobseekers looking for digital skills
freeDetails: Free digital-skills courses, with some learning routes promoted as zero-rated for Vodacom users.
requirements: Registration is usually required; data benefits may depend on network and current programme rules.
watchOut: Confirm the current course catalogue and any zero-rated data wording on Vodacom's own page.

name: Book Dash
bestFor: Parents, teachers, libraries and reading clubs
freeDetails: Free African children's picture books that can be read, downloaded, printed and shared under an open model.
requirements: No purchase needed for digital books; printing or data may still have normal user costs.
watchOut: Use the official Book Dash library rather than third-party download mirrors.

name: Nal'ibali
bestFor: Families, teachers and caregivers of children aged 0-12
freeDetails: Free stories, audio stories, printables and reading resources in South African languages.
requirements: No purchase needed for online resources; downloads and printing may use data or paper.
watchOut: Choose the language and age level that fits the child instead of downloading random PDFs.

name: FunDza
bestFor: Teens, young adults and mobile readers
freeDetails: Mobile-friendly South African stories, reading content and literacy resources.
requirements: Works best online or on mobile; account features may vary by current platform setup.
watchOut: It is stronger for teen and young-adult reading than preschool picture books.

name: Experian South Africa
bestFor: Consumers checking their credit report and score
freeDetails: Free access to Experian personal credit report and credit score through Experian's consumer platform.
requirements: Valid South African ID details and identity verification are expected.
watchOut: Use Experian's official site and never share banking passwords or card PINs.

name: TransUnion South Africa
bestFor: Consumers using their free annual bureau report right
freeDetails: Free annual credit report access from TransUnion South Africa.
requirements: Identity verification is required before report access.
watchOut: A free credit report is not the same as guaranteed credit approval.
manualOkReason: Official credit-bureau site can return automated-check 403 responses.
manualOkEvidence: Official TransUnion South Africa annual free credit report page was manually rechecked on 2026-06-26 for the consumer credit-report offer and matching URL.

name: ClearScore South Africa
bestFor: Consumers who want ongoing score and report visibility
freeDetails: Free credit score and report access, with personalised financial offers shown separately.
requirements: Sign-up and identity checks are required.
watchOut: Treat loan and credit offers as advertising or eligibility guidance, not approval.
manualOkReason: Official site can return automated-check 403 responses.
manualOkEvidence: Official ClearScore South Africa page was manually rechecked on 2026-06-26 as the consumer score/report entry point for South African users.

name: Brand Advisor
bestFor: Consumers interested in product testing and honest feedback
freeDetails: Product exchange and trial opportunities may be available when campaigns are open.
requirements: Application, profile details and content or feedback may be required.
watchOut: Campaigns are limited and not every application gets selected.

name: Review Club South Africa
bestFor: Creators and shoppers who can review products honestly
freeDetails: Free product campaigns may be offered to selected users in exchange for feedback or content.
requirements: Profile setup, campaign application and review/content tasks may apply.
watchOut: Check whether a campaign is active before signing up; availability can be limited.
manualOkReason: Official site may reject automated link-check requests.
manualOkEvidence: Official Review Club South Africa how-it-works page was manually rechecked on 2026-06-26 for free creator product campaign and product-testing programme context.

name: ReviewClub international product tests
bestFor: Users comparing how product-testing panels usually work
freeDetails: Explains product testing in exchange for honest feedback, reviews or surveys.
requirements: Selection, testing periods and feedback tasks vary by campaign.
watchOut: Use this as a product-testing explainer; always check whether a South African campaign is actually active.
